channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FDI Grew But Recovery Still Uneven — UN Warns
item.description: Foreign direct investment (FDI) increased by 6.0% in 2025 to $1.6 trillion, but the recovery remains uneven across countries, regions, and sectors, according to the UN Trade and Development agency (UN...

item.title: Supreme Court rejects DMK's attempt to restrict CM's comments on Karur stampede case
item.description: The Supreme Court recently dismissed a plea filed by the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) seeking to restrain Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay and other officials from making public statements...

item.title: Kenya's Agricultural Challenges: Low Fertilizer Use and Seed Adoption Hamper Productivity
item.description: Governor Irungu Kang'ata of Murang'a has highlighted that low fertilizer use and limited adoption of certified seeds are major barriers to agricultural productivity in Kenya. During a speech at the FI...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Continues Hearing Further Witness Testimony
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry continued its public hearings on Tuesday, with additional witness testimony forming part of its ongoing investigation into serious allegations involving South Africa...

item.title: JK Rowling offers legal funding after girl placed with older boys in secure unit
item.description: JK Rowling has offered to fund legal action following reports of a 13-year-old orphaned girl being housed in a secure unit alongside older teenage criminal boys. The vulnerable girl, transferred to th...

item.title: ACLU Allocates $25 Million to Protect Abortion and Voting Rights in State-Level Elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is allocating $25 million to bolster abortion and voting rights in the upcoming midterm elections, focusing on state-level races, legislative battles, and ref...

item.title: Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan strengthen bilateral ties through trade and regional collaboration
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari and Kyrgyz President Sadyr Japarov have pledged to deepen economic cooperation, enhance regional connectivity, and expand collaboration in sectors like tourism, IT, educatio...

item.title: Necta Cancels 58 Results After Students Use ChatGPT During Exams
item.description: The National Examinations Council of Tanzania (Necta) has canceled the results of 58 students who used ChatGPT during national exams, marking a significant crackdown on exam malpractice. The incident ...

item.title: Canada's Submarine Contract to Boost Halifax Economy
item.description: Canada's decision to award a new submarine contract to German company TKMS is set to deliver a significant economic boost to Halifax, according to officials. The deal, expected to be finalized by 2034...

item.title: East Africa's Role in the Indo-Pacific Geopolitical Shift
item.description: East Africa is emerging as a critical region in the Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape, with nations like Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda strategically positioning themselves amid growing Asian influence...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte's Threat Video Used in Impeachment Trial Against Marcos
item.description: The Senate impeachment court allowed the prosecution to present a two-minute portion of Vice President Sara Duterte's November 2024 press conference as evidence against President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr....

item.title: Safaricom Explains Process to Increase Fuliza Loan Limits via USSD
item.description: Safaricom has outlined the steps for Kenyan M-Pesa users to request higher Fuliza loan limits. The telco clarified that borrowers cannot expect automatic increases and must use the *334# USSD code to ...

item.title: Soccer's True Impact: Uniting South Africa Beyond the Pitch
item.description: The article argues that South Africa's traditional measure of soccer success—focused solely on qualification outcomes—oversimplifies the sport's broader societal value. It highlights how national socc...

item.title: PM Modi Receives Indonesia's Highest Honour Amid Strategic India-Indonesia Collaboration
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was conferred Indonesia's highest honour, the 'Bintang Adipurna' (Medal of Honour), during his visit to the country. This award recognizes exceptional service to Indonesia...

item.title: Governor Umahi highlights quality infrastructure to boost Nigeria's economy
item.description: Ebonyi State Governor David Umahi has assured the federal government's commitment to delivering high-quality and long-lasting road infrastructure to drive economic growth. During an inspection of the ...

item.title: Research Suggests Cut Soccer Socks Improve Comfort Perception Rather Than Athletic Performance
item.description: A growing number of professional soccer players at the 2026 FIFA World Cup have been seen wearing socks with holes cut into the calf area, prompting widespread speculation about whether the practice o...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Prosecution Seeks Her Testimony
item.description: The prosecution in Sara Duterte's impeachment trial has requested her appearance as a witness to address allegations of grave threats and incitement against the president. Private prosecutor Lorna Kap...

item.title: England's World Cup Triumph and Key Moments in Mexican Campaign
item.description: The article details England's historic World Cup victory over Mexico, highlighting Thomas Tuchel's tactical brilliance and Declan Rice's leadership as captain. It covers the team's 3-2 win in the Azte...

item.title: Landslide in Wayanad: Rescue operations underway after twin-tunnel construction site incident
item.description: A landslip occurred at the Anakkampoyil–Kalladi–Meppadi twin-tunnel road construction site near Meenakshi Bridge in Wayanad district on July 7, 2026. At least two workers were rescued and rushed to th...

item.title: South Korea Faces Criticism Over Detention of Elderly Church Leader in Political Case
item.description: South Korea has faced international criticism after detaining 95-year-old Shincheonji Church of Jesus chairman Lee Man-hee on charges related to a political party case. Prosecutors allege Lee organize...
